title: SpaceX en bolsa: ¿cuánto vale una compañía cuyo negocio depende de expectativas a muy largo plazo?
summary: SpaceX debutó en Nasdaq el 12 de junio de 2026 bajo el ticker SPCX a 135 dólares por acción, levantando 75,000 millones de dólares y alcanzando una valoración de 1.77 billones —la mayor OPI de la historia—, convirtiendo a Elon Musk en la primera persona con una fortuna personal superior al billón de dólares. El artículo examina el desafío de valorar una empresa cuyo modelo de negocio depende de expectativas de muy largo plazo, simbolizando la transición de la exploración espacial financiada por Estados a un ecosistema donde el capital privado busca rentabilidad en actividades antes consideradas ciencia ficción. Los analistas debaten si la valuación actual refleja los flujos de caja reales o una apuesta especulativa sobre horizontes de décadas.

title: ¿Comprar o rentar vivienda en México? Las claves para tomar una decisión financiera inteligente
summary: El precio promedio de una vivienda en México superó los 2 millones de pesos en el primer trimestre de 2026 según la Sociedad Hipotecaria Federal, requiriendo ingresos mensuales de aproximadamente 83,500 pesos para acceder a un crédito hipotecario, elevando considerablemente la barrera de entrada a la propiedad. El artículo analiza cómo el alza sostenida de precios inmobiliarios, la mayor movilidad laboral y las nuevas formas de trabajo están desafiando la lógica histórica de que comprar siempre supera a rentar. Especialistas apuntan que la decisión óptima depende del horizonte de permanencia, la capacidad de enganche y la etapa de vida del comprador, no de una regla universal.

title: Agendas llenas, vidas vacías
summary: Salvador Alva, expresidente del Tecnológico de Monterrey y de PepsiCo en América Latina, advierte en su libro "Vidas plenas" que los ejecutivos acumulan actividades y compromisos a un ritmo que vacía el sentido de propósito y la dirección estratégica personal. El columnista Alberto Tovar destaca que el capitalismo genera opciones y gratificación inmediata pero también una sensación permanente de insuficiencia que lleva a trabajar para aparentar en lugar de construir. El texto es un llamado a replantear los indicadores de avance personal más allá de métricas externas de productividad, relevante para líderes que gestionan equipos en transformación.

title: AI's Energy Crunch Has Investors Searching for Next IPO Winners
summary: The AI boom's insatiable power demand is generating a new wave of investment and IPO activity in the energy and infrastructure sector, with Wall Street betting billions on companies promising to solve the electricity shortfall — even when underlying technologies remain unproven. Infrastructure providers positioned to serve hyperscale data centers are attracting record valuations as AI model training and inference continue to scale beyond current grid capacity. The dynamic mirrors earlier semiconductor euphoria, with investors front-running the energy constraint before revenue streams for these new entrants fully materialize.

title: Tech Slump Deepens
summary: Technology stocks closed sharply lower to cap a volatile week as investors reassessed the sustainability of the AI trade amid concerns over rising semiconductor costs, memory pricing pressures, and capital spending levels that may not be justified by near-term revenues. US restrictions on advanced AI model exports added further uncertainty, while the cooling IPO market raised questions about whether AI companies can generate sufficient returns to justify elevated valuations. Bloomberg Intelligence analysts flagged a structural shift underway: broad AI enthusiasm is giving way to selectivity, and evolving regulatory constraints are becoming a core variable in AI equity analysis.

title: Building Public Life: How Bogotá and Mexico City Addressed Urban Inequality
summary: Urban design projects in Bogotá and Mexico City have shifted focus from traditional infrastructure to spaces integrating education, culture, recreation, and community services in peripheral neighborhoods historically underserved by public investment. Mexico City's PILARES program — including the Cuicuilco Community Center — exemplifies how multi-functional civic buildings can reduce the quality-of-life gap between wealthy and working-class urban zones. The article argues that building public life, not just public services, is a more durable strategy for addressing spatial inequality across Latin American cities.

title: Utility boss warns US faces blackouts due to power supply shortfall
summary: Exelon's chief executive warned that the United States risks widespread blackouts because the electricity grid cannot keep pace with surging demand driven by the AI boom and rapid data center expansion. Electricity prices will need to rise materially to fund the infrastructure buildout required to avoid supply shortfalls, framing rate increases as a necessary cost of the AI era. The warning underscores growing consensus that AI's physical infrastructure requirements — particularly power — represent a binding constraint on the technology's continued scaling.

title: Trump administration allows some access to Anthropic's Mythos
summary: The Trump administration granted limited access to Anthropic's most advanced AI model, Mythos, easing tensions with the AI lab after a national security review but stopping short of unrestricted deployment. While the agreement moves toward normalcy for one of the frontier AI labs, observers note that Washington's ad hoc regulatory approach creates persistent uncertainty for developers of the most capable AI systems. The episode is shaping up as a precedent-setting test case for how the US government will manage ongoing oversight of frontier AI models without a formal statutory framework.

title: Anthropic's Mythos 5 AI Model Cleared by US for Wider Use
summary: Anthropic secured US government approval to restore broader access to its Mythos 5 artificial intelligence model after resolving Trump administration concerns about the technology's potential national security risks. The clearance applies to trusted partners and marks a significant milestone in the negotiation between frontier AI labs and Washington over deployment standards and access controls for the most capable AI systems. The resolution follows weeks of talks focused on security protocols and signals an emerging model for how the federal government intends to manage access to leading-edge AI outside of formal regulatory legislation.

title: Forum AI CEO on Pitfalls With AI in Politics
summary: Campbell Brown, Co-Founder and CEO of Forum AI, argues that AI companies must open themselves to greater public scrutiny, particularly as the technology increasingly shapes political information environments. Speaking on Bloomberg's "The Close," she addressed the pitfalls of AI in politics and the accountability gap facing the industry in the current regulatory landscape. Her call for transparency reflects growing practitioner concern about AI-generated content and data-driven political tools outpacing governance mechanisms.

title: Anthropic Moves Toward Deal With US to Lift Curbs on AI Models
summary: Anthropic and the Trump administration were nearing an agreement to lift US restrictions on the company's two most advanced AI models, following weeks of negotiation over national security concerns and system safety protocols, according to people familiar with the matter. The deal would allow wider deployment of models constrained due to concerns about misuse and potential foreign access to frontier capabilities. The negotiations reflect a broader dynamic in which leading AI labs must navigate active government oversight as a condition of operating at scale — a precedent with significant implications for the entire industry.

title: AI Trade's Bruising Week Forces Investors to Be More Selective
summary: A sharp selloff in AI-exposed equities — particularly semiconductor stocks — forced institutional investors to rethink broad AI exposure and pivot toward selective, higher-conviction positions in companies with clear near-term revenue visibility. The week's market action signals a maturing of the AI investment thesis: diffuse enthusiasm for anything AI-adjacent is giving way to differentiation between infrastructure enablers with tangible demand and speculative plays on future adoption curves. Fintech and early-stage AI startup valuations are being reassessed under the same lens as investors apply tighter capital discipline.

title: Nasdaq y S&P 500 suman cinco días de pérdidas, mientras se acelera la rotación fuera de la tecnología
summary: El S&P 500 y el Nasdaq acumularon cinco caídas consecutivas lideradas por una venta masiva en fabricantes de semiconductores —tras un trimestre de ganancias históricas— evidenciando una rotación sectorial hacia valores más amplios del mercado. El índice ponderado por igual del S&P 500 tocó máximos históricos en paralelo, señal de que el impulso bursátil se extiende más allá de las grandes tecnológicas y el trade concentrado de IA. Analistas interpretan el movimiento como un ajuste técnico del posicionamiento en IA, no como una ruptura estructural del ciclo alcista, aunque la selectividad ahora es clave.

title: Beyond Chips, IMF Sees AI Wealth Boom Adding to Inflation Risks
summary: The IMF's chief economist warned that artificial intelligence could drive inflation through two distinct channels: the well-understood surge in chip and semiconductor costs, and a less-discussed wealth effect in which AI-driven productivity gains make consumers wealthier and more willing to spend, adding demand-side price pressure. The analysis complicates the inflation outlook for central banks, suggesting that AI adoption may require monetary models to account for AI-driven wealth accumulation as a novel inflationary variable. This marks a shift in how multilateral institutions frame AI's macroeconomic risk — from a deflationary labor-substitution story to a more complex, dual-sided picture.

title: Apple's Vision Pro and Smart Glasses Chief to Join OpenAI
summary: Apple's top executive overseeing the Vision Pro headset and the company's smart glasses program is departing to join OpenAI, the latest in a series of high-profile talent defections from Apple and other hardware companies to frontier AI labs. The move signals OpenAI's deepening ambitions in AI-integrated physical hardware at a time when the smart glasses market — catalyzed by Meta's Ray-Ban collaboration — is gaining meaningful consumer traction. The departure raises questions about Apple's mixed reality roadmap while reinforcing OpenAI's competitive positioning as a company increasingly competing in AI-native device interfaces, not just software.

title: Villa Riviera Golf Residence / Atelier130
summary: Atelier130 completed a 600 m² private golf residence in Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire in 2025, integrating tropical architecture with a lifestyle centered on golf and premium outdoor living. The project exemplifies the growing segment of high-end leisure residences anchored around golf amenities in emerging urban markets, where demand for resort-quality private homes is expanding. Photographs by Alejandra Loreto showcase the refined integration of interior comfort with resort-style outdoor spaces across the 600 m² footprint.

title: OpenAI Limits Release of New Model Under Pressure From US
summary: OpenAI is deploying a preview of its latest, more capable AI model exclusively to select trusted partners before broader public release, following direct pressure from the Trump administration to stagger the rollout and allow time for national security and safety review. The controlled release strategy reflects an emerging pattern in which frontier AI labs coordinate deployment timelines with government officials, even absent formal legislation mandating such reviews. The administration's influence over private AI model release schedules raises fundamental questions about the boundaries of government oversight over commercially developed AI systems.

title: AI Is Already Reshaping US Politics at Every Level
summary: Silicon Valley capital and AI technology are pervasively reshaping the 2026 US midterm elections, from data center siting generating local backlash to tech billionaires funding campaigns and AI-generated deepfakes appearing in political advertising. The article documents how AI's political footprint has expanded far beyond micro-targeting, now influencing candidate recruitment, voter file analysis, and media narrative formation at every level of government. The pace of AI adoption in democratic processes is outstripping regulatory frameworks, raising governance questions about accountability when AI shapes electoral outcomes.
